title: The Return of Large Mammals
text:
Populations of wolves, foxes, Eurasian lynx, elk and wild boar have significantly increased here. Species such as brown bears and European bison, meanwhile, have returned.

This is rewilding in its most extreme form, given the inability of humans to intervene and it has resulted in several unexpected effects in the CEZ.

Studies indicate that the lack of human hunting, agriculture and development has a more positive impact on animal numbers than radiation has a negative one.

Large mammal populations in the Belarusian sector of the zone are comparable to or higher than those in uncontaminated nature reserves.

There is no doubt that initial radiation caused major damage to flora and fauna, most notably in the Red Forest, a 10 km² area near the nuclear power plant.

This area earned its name after pine trees died and turned red-brown due to high radiation absorption. Yet long-term studies show that biodiversity has increased in the absence of humans.

quizlet: rewilding in its most extreme form
восстановление природы в наиболее экстремальной форме
rewilding in its most extreme form

questions.question: Which animals have significantly increased in the Chernobyl Exclusion Zone?
options: Wolves, foxes, Eurasian lynx, elk and wild boar
Lions, elephants and giraffes
Penguins, seals and whales
Only domestic animals
questions.explanation: The passage lists wolves, foxes, Eurasian lynx, elk and wild boar as species whose populations have significantly increased.

questions.question: Why is Chernobyl described as rewilding in its most extreme form?
options: Because humans are unable to intervene in the area
Because all animals were artificially introduced
Because radiation was deliberately used to protect wildlife
Because the area is managed like a zoo
questions.explanation: The text says this is rewilding in its most extreme form because humans cannot intervene.

questions.question: What do studies indicate about animal numbers in the zone?
options: Radiation has a more positive effect than human absence
Human hunting increases biodiversity
The lack of hunting, agriculture and development has a strong positive impact
Animal numbers are lower than everywhere else
questions.explanation: The passage says that the lack of human hunting, agriculture and development has a more positive impact on animal numbers than radiation has a negative one.

questions.question: What was the Red Forest named after?
options: A rare red bird species
Pine trees that died and turned red-brown after absorbing radiation
Red soil brought from Belarus
A conservation project
questions.explanation: The Red Forest earned its name after pine trees died and turned red-brown due to high radiation absorption.

questions.question: What do long-term studies show about biodiversity in the zone?
options: It has increased in the absence of humans
It has completely disappeared
It has remained exactly the same
It only increased inside cities
questions.explanation: The text says long-term studies show that biodiversity has increased in the absence of humans.
